      <title>Steve Kerr Decided To Retire As Warriors Coach Before Changing His Mind After Play-In Win</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285634/Steve-Kerr-Decided-To-Retire-As-Warriors-Coach-Before-Changing-His-Mind-After-Play-In-Win</link>
      <description><p>Steve Kerr had made up his mind he was going to retire as Golden State's head coach after 12 seasons regardless of the outcome of their play-in game against the Los Angeles Clippers in April. Kerr put the odds at 95%, but then the Warriors won, and everything changed.</p>
<p>"I'm not leaving," Kerr told his staff in the locker room after the Clippers victory.  Kerr's wife, Margot Kerr, sent him a text that said the same thing.</p>
<p>The reversal came after months of genuine internal conflict. As far back as June 2025, Kerr had begun discussing retirement with his wife. He sensed the dynasty had run its course and worried about overstaying his welcome, having watched mentor Gregg Popovich struggle to walk away from the San Antonio Spurs before a stroke ultimately made the decision for him.</p>
<p>"I realized he couldn't do it," Kerr said of Popovich. "He couldn't walk away."</p>
<p>By the time the Warriors reached the play-in round, Kerr had grown increasingly certain his time was done. He told staff privately his decision was nearly final. But the victory over the Clippers, a furious comeback fueled by Al Horford hitting four straight three-pointers and Stephen Curry making the decisive play, reminded him of everything he stood to leave behind.</p>
<p>The following week he drove to Curry's home in Atherton to get his star player's read on the situation. He met with owner Joe Lacob and general manager Mike Dunleavy. His daughter Maddy scolded him for dwelling on what he didn't like about the job rather than the hundred things he did. Margot wanted him to stay, partly because it kept their son Nick and his family close by.</p>
<p>The competing emotional pulls were real and he acknowledged them directly.</p>
<p>"Coaching has unlocked the best version of myself," Kerr said. "I think I'm scared that I will lose that daily engagement and purpose that not only feeds my soul, but helps me deal with my literal chronic daily pain."</p>
<p>Lacob told Kerr he wanted him back. The two men, who had disagreed on some things during the season, shared a conviction that what the Warriors had built was worth protecting and fighting for. Kerr told Dunleavy he was in.</p>
<p>Kerr called Curry with the news and the two spoke about the road ahead. Draymond Green went on the "Inside the NBA" set and echoed the themes Kerr had outlined in a personal letter he had written to Green during a difficult stretch mid-season, a letter centered on loyalty, fighting spirit and the obligation to set the franchise up for success when the current core was gone.</p>
<p>Kerr laughed when he reflected on the full arc of his deliberations.</p>
<p>"I'm just like Riley," he said, referencing Pat Riley's famous inability to step away from the game.</p>
<p>Kerr had fielded numerous offers through his agent, including opportunities to become a team president or a return to broadcasting. </p>
<p>"I'm not a suit," Kerr said of running a team's front office as he did previously with the Phoenix Suns. "I want the whistle."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 12:53:35 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285634/Steve-Kerr-Decided-To-Retire-As-Warriors-Coach-Before-Changing-His-Mind-After-Play-In-Win</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Kerr_Steve_gsw_240112.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Adam Silver Says NBA's 'System Of Flat Odds' Will Give Teams 'No Particular Incentive To Be Bad'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285624/Adam-Silver-Says-NBAs-System-Of-Flat-Odds-Will-Give-Teams-No-Particular-Incentive-To-Be-Bad</link>
      <description><p>NBA commissioner Adam Silver confirmed Wednesday that the league has finalized a draft lottery reform proposal to present to team owners at the end of May, designed to eliminate the competitive incentive to lose games deliberately. The proposal centers on a flat-odds system that would reduce the advantage of finishing with the league's worst record.</p>
<p>"We've essentially done, and we have a proposal that we're going to be bringing to our team owners at the end of May, and that is to create essentially a system of flat odds, so that you have no particular incentive to be bad," Silver said on Stephen A. Smith's radio show.</p>
<p>The proposal matches what ESPN first reported as the "3-2-1" system. Under the structure, the three worst teams in the league would actually receive lower lottery odds than clubs finishing fourth through tenth, a deliberate reversal of the current incentive structure. Silver described the concept as "draft relegation."</p>
<p>"If you're one of the bottom three teams in the league, you'll actually have worse odds than teams that sort of are four through up until teams make the playoffs," Silver said. "We're still playing a little bit with the system there."</p>
<p>The reform would also expand the league's disciplinary authority over teams deemed to be deliberately losing. The NBA fined the Utah Jazz $500,000 for conduct detrimental to the league earlier this season, but Silver indicated future penalties could go further.</p>
<p>"Teams have to know it's not just about paying a financial fine, which they may think is worth it in order to get a top pick, but that it'll directly impact their ability to get a top draft pick," Silver said. "We can actually take away draft lottery balls, we can change the order of the draft."</p>
<p>All four teams that jumped up in this season's lottery, the Washington Wizards, Utah Jazz, Memphis Grizzlies and Chicago Bulls, made moves in the final weeks of the regular season aimed at improving their draft positioning. League officials told ESPN the expectation is the proposal will pass this month.</p>
<p>The system is designed as a three-year measure set to expire in 2029 when the new collective bargaining agreement takes effect, at which point tanking rules could be renegotiated.</p>
<p>"The teams are incredibly innovative and creative at coming up with ways to work the system," Silver said. "This will give us time for additional study to see whether there are other creative ways to better distribute players."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285606/NBAs-New-Lottery-Reform-Expected-To-Make-Teams-More-Reluctant-To-Trade-Future-Firsts">There is an expectation amongst NBA front offices that teams will be less willing to trade future picks under the new system</a> due to the increased probability of a higher draft pick regardless of record.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 07:22:11 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285624/Adam-Silver-Says-NBAs-System-Of-Flat-Odds-Will-Give-Teams-No-Particular-Incentive-To-Be-Bad</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Silver_Adam_nba_240331.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Bulls Hire Stephen Mervis As SVP Of Basketball Ops; Acie Law As VP Of Player Personnel</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285631/Bulls-Hire-Stephen-Mervis-As-SVP-Of-Basketball-Ops;-Acie-Law-As-VP-Of-Player-Personnel</link>
      <description><p>Stephen Mervis has been hired by the Chicago Bulls as Senior Vice President of Basketball Operations under new Executive Vice President Bryson Graham. </p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/staff/Stephen-Mervis/Summary/12268">Mervis was most recently assistant general manager of the Orlando Magic</a> and will slot in as Graham's top lieutenant. Mervis began his career with the Indiana Pacers during the 2013-14 season before joining the Magic in 2014-15.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285477/Bulls-Pursuing-Celtics-Assistant-GM-Dave-Lewin-For-Front-Office-Role">The Bulls were also interested in hiring Dave Lewin for the job</a> after they were impressed with him during the interview process for the top position that went to Graham.</p>
<p>Chicago will also hire Acie Law as Vice President of Player Personnel. Law and Graham played collegiately together at Texas A&M.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 12:15:11 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285631/Bulls-Hire-Stephen-Mervis-As-SVP-Of-Basketball-Ops;-Acie-Law-As-VP-Of-Player-Personnel</guid>

      <title>NBA Soft-Tissue Injuries Have Surged As Players Try To Keep Up With Demands Of Modern Game</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285638/NBA-Soft-Tissue-Injuries-Have-Surged-As-Players-Try-To-Keep-Up-With-Demands-Of-Modern-Game</link>
      <description><p>Soft-tissue leg injuries have been one of the key developments of the 2026 NBA playoffs, as they were last year, and the numbers behind the trend are striking. Documented calf injuries across the entire league stood at 18 in 2010-11. Last season, that figure reached 60 and 86 this season. </p>
<p>Jalen Williams has missed six Oklahoma City Thunder playoff games with a hamstring strain. Franz Wagner, OG Anunoby, Aaron Gordon, Peyton Watson, and Jayson Tatum have all missed time with calf or hamstring issues. Donte DiVincenzo ruptured his Achilles. Luka Doncic missed the Los Angeles Lakers' entire playoff run with a Grade 2 hamstring tear. Anthony Edwards has been limited since returning from a knee injury.</p>
<p>Researchers and sports scientists point to a fundamental shift in how basketball is played. Ron Adams, a longtime NBA assistant coach, framed the issue directly.</p>
<p>"Basketball used to be a two-footed sport," said Adams. "If you ever watched a John Wooden practice, it was always the same: get to the paint and play off of two feet. Nowadays, the game is a one-footed sport. Most players are making every move off of one foot."</p>
<p>The stepback three and the Euro-step, now central to modern NBA offense, place extreme demand on the calf and hamstring. Richard Lieber, chief scientific officer at the Shirley Ryan AbilityLab and author of the standard textbook on skeletal muscle, describes the core mechanism: muscle damage occurs when a muscle is both activated and stretched simultaneously. The stepback does exactly that, forcing rapid eccentric loading on a single leg.</p>
<p>The star players most associated with one-footed creation have paid a physical cost. Jayson Tatum attempted 11 stepback threes in his 2017-18 rookie season. In 2024-25, he attempted 251, a 23-fold rise. Only five players have surpassed 1,000 career stepback threes since tracking began in 2013-14: James Harden, Doncic, Stephen Curry, Tatum, and Damian Lillard. Lillard and Tatum have each suffered Achilles tears. Doncic has sustained three calf strains. Curry recorded the first muscle strain of his 17-year career in last year's playoffs.</p>
<p>The recurring-injury numbers are particularly concerning. This season, 13 players sustained multiple calf injuries. Six players suffered three or more calf strains in a single season: Coby White, Rui Hachimura, Isaiah Hartenstein, Giannis Antetokounmpo, Evan Mobley, and Ty Jerome. From 2010 through 2024, that had happened a total of five times across 14 seasons combined. Calf injuries now account for nearly one in four lower-leg injuries league-wide, up from fewer than one in ten a decade ago. Last season, six players tore their Achilles, the highest single-season total on record.</p>
<p>The league's schedule compounds the strain. An 82-game regular season feeds into a two-month playoff run, with no meaningful structural change to reduce physical wear. The NBA introduced an in-season tournament in 2023 and added a 65-game eligibility threshold for award consideration, moves that increase competitive pressure rather than reduce it. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 17:54:09 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285638/NBA-Soft-Tissue-Injuries-Have-Surged-As-Players-Try-To-Keep-Up-With-Demands-Of-Modern-Game</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Tatum_Jayson_250503.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>AJ Dybantsa Addresses Viral Reaction To Wizards Winning Lottery</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285618/AJ-Dybantsa-Addresses-Viral-Reaction-To-Wizards-Winning-Lottery</link>
      <description><p>AJ Dybantsa, <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285562/ESPN-Athletic-Have-Identical-Top-10-In-Post-Lottery-NBA-Mock-Drafts">the current most probable No. 1 pick in next month's NBA Draft</a>, is approaching the week-long combine as an extended audition. The BYU wing showed up to his 20-minute media session Wednesday in a gray plaid suit, dark blue dress shirt and blue-accented tie, a deliberate departure from the casual attire most prospects wear to combine interviews.</p>
<p>"I never had a job before," Dybantsa told reporters. "I was 13 when I started taking basketball serious. But this is like my first job interview. So my dad's kind of like, 'You know, this is your job interview. So come professional, come in a suit.'"</p>
<p>Dybantsa said he had already met with the Washington Wizards, Utah Jazz, Memphis Grizzlies, Chicago Bulls, Los Angeles Clippers, Atlanta Hawks and Dallas Mavericks by midday Wednesday. The Wizards hold the top pick following last month's draft lottery.</p>
<p>Dybantsa addressed the viral moment after Washington won the lottery, when his stone-faced reaction prompted speculation he did not want to play for the Wizards.</p>
<p>"I literally had no reaction at all, and people find ways to say that I didn't want it. I did want it," Dybantsa said. "But it all comes with social media. People are going to make narratives. I just tried not to react at all, just keep a straight face, try to stay even-keeled."</p>
<p>When asked why he should be the top selection, Dybantsa pointed to his two-way versatility and scoring ability.</p>
<p>"I think I can guard one through four, play one through four," he said. "I play the game the right way. But I'm super exciting as a player, super explosive. I fill seats."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 13 May 2026 22:48:35 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285618/AJ-Dybantsa-Addresses-Viral-Reaction-To-Wizards-Winning-Lottery</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Dybantsa_AJ_byu_251212.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Darryn Peterson Views Himself As Point Guard But Willing To Play Any Role For NBA Team</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285620/Darryn-Peterson-Views-Himself-As-Point-Guard-But-Willing-To-Play-Any-Role-For-NBA-Team</link>
      <description><p>Kansas guard Darryn Peterson pushed back Wednesday on speculation that he would rule out teams unwilling to play him as a primary point guard, telling reporters at the NBA Draft Combine he is prepared to contribute in any role.</p>
<p>Peterson had previously drawn attention after comments suggesting he performs best running an offense, prompting questions about whether positional fit would factor into his draft preferences. He was direct in his response.</p>
<p>"Nah, no, sir," Peterson said when asked if he would cross a team off his list for viewing him as a two-guard. "At the end of the day, whoever wants to pick you is probably going to pick you. So if they come in and say I'm a two-guard, I'm familiar with it now from playing at Kansas, so I'll just make a way."</p>
<p>Peterson said he had met with approximately 10 teams by midday Wednesday, including the Washington Wizards, Utah Jazz and Memphis Grizzlies. He described his value as a versatile offensive weapon capable of operating with or without the ball.</p>
<p>"I think I can fit with any team just because I think I can play both off and on the ball and help my team wherever I go," he said.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 13 May 2026 23:00:26 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285620/Darryn-Peterson-Views-Himself-As-Point-Guard-But-Willing-To-Play-Any-Role-For-NBA-Team</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Peterson_Darryn_ku_251105.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jalen Brunson Deflects 'Star' Question When Discussing Acceptance Of Off-Ball Role</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285637/Jalen-Brunson-Deflects-Star-Question-When-Discussing-Acceptance-Of-Off-Ball-Role</link>
      <description><p>Jalen Brunson immediately accepted a reduced ball-handling role when coach Mike Brown proposed running more offense through Karl-Anthony Towns during the playoffs, a shift that has since produced seven consecutive New York Knicks victories.</p>
<p>The adjustment came after New York dropped two straight games to the Atlanta Hawks in their first round series, putting the team in danger of an early postseason exit. Brown approached players about redistributing offensive responsibilities, with Brunson moving off the ball more frequently.</p>
<p>"Was there any concern? No. What was the dialogue? The dialogue was, 'OK, let's do it,'" Brunson said Wednesday. "It was that simple. There's not really much you can talk about at that point when you're down 2-1."</p>
<p>When a reporter suggested other star players might have resisted, Brunson replied: "1) I'm not a star. 2) I want to win." He added while leaving: "I'm not self-centered, that's why."</p>
<p>New York has outscored opponents by an average of 26.4 points during the streak, posting a 130.5 offensive rating per 100 possessions. Towns has recorded 66 postseason assists, 44 more than his total from a year ago.</p>
<p>Brunson's own production has climbed as well. He is averaging 27.3 points on 51.9 percent shooting and 42.6 percent from three during the streak. His scoring efficiency per touch rose from .285 during the regular season to .324.</p>
<p>"Putting KAT in that position knowing we have great screeners and great cutters, and like I said a great facilitator, it was easier to say, 'Let's try it and do it more,'" Brown said.</p>
<p>Brunson's buy-in mirrors his 2024 contract extension, a four-year, $156.5 million deal in which he accepted less than market value, helping New York avoid the second apron and strengthen its roster depth.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 15:18:01 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285637/Jalen-Brunson-Deflects-Star-Question-When-Discussing-Acceptance-Of-Off-Ball-Role</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Brunson_Jalen_nyk_260501.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Keegan Murray Underwent Left Ankle Surgery</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285625/Keegan-Murray-Underwent-Left-Ankle-Surgery</link>
      <description><p>Sacramento Kings forward Keegan Murray underwent left ankle surgery on Tuesday. The procedure was to remove loose bodies in Murray's ankle. The Kings said Murray will be re-evaluated in six to eight weeks.</p>
<p>Last season was a lost one for Murray. The fourth-year forward appeared in only 23 games due to a ligament tear in his left thumb, then repeated sprains of his left ankle. Murray's final game was on February 25, as he was shut down for the season shortly thereafter due to the ankle issues.</p>
<p>This coming season, Murray will start the first year of the five-year, $140 million extension he signed with Sacramento this past offseason. The Kings still see Murray as one of their building blocks moving forward, despite the injury-plagued last season.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 08:57:38 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285625/Keegan-Murray-Underwent-Left-Ankle-Surgery</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Murray_Keegan_sac_221114.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Jalen Duren Takes Accountability For Play Amid Late Benching In Game 5</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285633/Jalen-Duren-Takes-Accountability-For-Play-Amid-Late-Benching-In-Game-5</link>
      <description><p>Jalen Duren has struggled during the Detroit Pistons' playoff run and the situation came to a head in the fourth quarter of Game 5 when he was benched by J.B. Bickerstaff in favor of Paul Reed Jr.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/stats/2026/Advanced_Stats/Qualified/points/All/desc/1/Playoffs">Duren has a PER of 13.4 during the playoffs</a> compared to 25.8 during the regular season in what will likely be an All-NBA award ahead of his restricted free agency.</p>
<p>Duren took accountability for his play when speaking to the media postgame.</p>
<p>"I feel I can be better in all aspects of the game," said Duren. "Continue to grind and continue to grow."</p>
<p>Duren was supportive of Reed and ultimately just wants the Pistons to win.</p>
<p>"As long as we get the win, I'm good," said Duren. "Obviously it didn't come out the way we want it today."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 12:28:02 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285633/Jalen-Duren-Takes-Accountability-For-Play-Amid-Late-Benching-In-Game-5</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Duren_Jalen_det_251210.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Makar Gevorkian Promoted By Nets To Assistant GM</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285630/Makar-Gevorkian-Promoted-By-Nets-To-Assistant-GM</link>
      <description><p>Makar Gevorkian has been promoted by the Brooklyn Nets to assistant general manager. Gevorkian was elevated to the role following two seasons as vice president of basketball operations alignment & strategic planning.</p>
<p>"Makar's strategic, forward-thinking mind, along with his comprehensive knowledge of the league's salary cap structure, have made him a valued voice within our front office and we are excited to elevate him to this well-earned role," said Nets General Manager Sean Marks.</p>
<p>Recently completing his sixth season with the Nets, Gevorkian joined the franchise in 2020 as a basketball operations assistant. He went on to serve one season as a salary cap and strategy associate and two seasons as the director of salary cap before being promoted to his most recent position in 2024. Gevorkian earned his law degree from the University of Chicago and was admitted to the California Bar after graduating from Loyola Marymount University with a bachelor's degree in economics and a minor in applied mathematics.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 12:06:07 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285630/Makar-Gevorkian-Promoted-By-Nets-To-Assistant-GM</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_brooklynnets.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Ebuka Okorie, Henri Veesaar, Isaiah Evans, Jack Kayil Staying In Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285627/Ebuka-Okorie-Henri-Veesaar-Isaiah-Evans-Jack-Kayil-Staying-In-Draft</link>
      <description><p>As the 2026 NBA Draft Combine progresses this week, several Early Entry Candidates are making decisions on their future. Ebuka Okorie of Stanford, Henri Veesaar of North Carolina, Isaiah Evans of Duke and Germany's Jack Kayil have all shared that they are staying in the 2026 NBA Draft.</p>
<p>Okorie averaged 23.2 points, 3.6 rebounds, 3.6 assists and 1.6 steals as a freshman for the Cardinal. The 6-foot-2 points guard shot 46.5% from the field, 35.4% on three-pointers and 83.2% on free throws.</p>
<p>Veesaar had a breakout season for the Tar Heels as a junior. The 7-foot big man out of Estonia averaged 17 points, 8.7 rebounds, 2.1 assists and 1.2 blocks per game. Veesaar also shot 61% from the field, including 42.6% on 3.0 three-point attempts per game.</p>
<p>Evans doubled his production across the board in his sophomore season with the Blue Devils. The 6-foot-6 wing averaged 15 points and 3.2 rebounds per game, while shooting 36% on 7.4 three-point attempts per game.</p>
<p>Kayil spent last season with Alba Berlin in Germany, after playing the previous year in Serbia. The 6-foot-5 guard averaged 12 points, 2.9 rebounds and 3.7 assists in 50 games across all competitions.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 09:48:01 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285627/Ebuka-Okorie-Henri-Veesaar-Isaiah-Evans-Jack-Kayil-Staying-In-Draft</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_realgm2.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Allen Graves To Stay In Draft If Considered First Rounder; Will Consider LSU, Duke If Returning To NCAA</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285626/Allen-Graves-To-Stay-In-Draft-If-Considered-First-Rounder;-Will-Consider-LSU-Duke-If-Returning-To-NCAA</link>
      <description><p>Santa Clara forward Allen Graves is focused on excelling at the NBA Draft Combine this week. If Graves is considered a likely first-round pick, he's going to stay in the 2026 NBA Draft. If the freshman forward chooses to return to college, he's considering LSU and Duke. Graves mentioned that he's also heard from Kentucky, but that the Tigers and Blue Devils are his top choices.</p>
<p>The 6-foot-9 forward excelled as a redshirt freshman for the Broncos last season. In 35 games. Graves averaged 11.8 points, 6.5 rebounds, 1.9 steals and nearly a block per game. Graves shot 51% from the field, 41% from behind the arc and 75% at the free throw line.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 09:26:33 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285626/Allen-Graves-To-Stay-In-Draft-If-Considered-First-Rounder;-Will-Consider-LSU-Duke-If-Returning-To-NCAA</guid>

   <item>
      <title>Fever-Wings Matchup Averages 2.49M Viewers On ABC</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285623/Fever-Wings-Matchup-Averages-249M-Viewers-On-ABC</link>
      <description><p>Saturday's WNBA matchup between Caitlin Clark's Indiana Fever and Paige Bueckers' Dallas Wings averaged 2.49 million viewers on ABC.</p>
<p>It is also the second most-watched WNBA regular season game on ESPN or ABC after last year's season opener between the Fever and the Sky.</p>
<p>The three most-viewed WNBA games on ABC and ESPN all feature Clark.</p>
<p>The second game of the doubleheader between Las Vegas and Phoenix averaged 1.15 million viewers.</p>
<p>The doubleheader averaged 1.9 million viewers, making it the second most-watched opening weekend for ABC and ESPN since 1997.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Kenny Atkinson Credits 'Mental Toughness' For Cavs' Overtime Win In Game 5</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285622/Kenny-Atkinson-Credits-Mental-Toughness-For-Cavs-Overtime-Win-In-Game-5</link>
      <description><p>The Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Detroit Pistons 117-113 in overtime in Game 5 to take a 3-2 series lead in the Eastern Conference semifinals.</p>
<p>The Cavs were down 103-94 in the fourth quarter before scoring nine straight points to force overtime. </p>
<p>It was also the first Cavs' road win of the playoffs.</p>
<p>"That stretch right there says a lot about our progress," Cavs coach Kenny Atkinson said. "[Our] mental toughness progress ... these guys, they never get down."</p>
<p>The Pistons had a 96.8% chance to win the game when they were up nine, according to ESPN analytics.</p>
<p>James Harden lead the Cavs with 30 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists, with Donovan Mitchell adding 21 points. Max Strus scored 20 points off the bench.</p>
<p>Cade Cunningham scored 39 points for the Pistons. Replacing Duncan Robinson in the starting lineup, Daniss Jenkins added 19 points.</p>
<p>Donovan Mitchell said that the Cavs needed to keep their composure ahead of Friday's Game 6.</p>
<p>"The biggest thing is just not get too high, get too low," said Mitchell. "At the end of the day, it's 3-2. The series is not over by any means. It was great to get one on the road. Yay, whoop-de-do. But we got to protect home court."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">The Cavaliers are 3.5 point favorites in Game 6 back in Cleveland on Friday</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Thu, 14 May 2026 02:20:52 EDT</pubDate>

   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Caleb Wilson Says Dual Hand Injuries Gave Him New Perspective Heading Into NBA Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285621/Caleb-Wilson-Says-Dual-Hand-Injuries-Gave-Him-New-Perspective-Heading-Into-NBA-Draft</link>
      <description><p>North Carolina forward Caleb Wilson arrived at the NBA Draft Combine this week with both hands fully healthy and a renewed appreciation for the game after a season derailed by two separate fractures.</p>
<p>Wilson broke a bone in his left hand on February 10 when he fell attempting to block a shot, then fractured his right thumb on March 5 after striking the rim during practice. The injuries limited him to just 24 games for the Tar Heels.</p>
<p>"I never really felt like my hand injury was a setback," Wilson told reporters Wednesday. "Of course, I was heartbroken when it happened. But my hand's perfectly fine now, and I feel like it offered me a different perspective on basketball in general. Now I'm way more grateful to play. Every day I wake up and I get the opportunity to play and just be able to shoot and dribble and stuff like that."</p>
<p>Wilson said he has interviewed with the Utah Jazz, Chicago Bulls, Los Angeles Clippers, Brooklyn Nets and Charlotte Hornets, with a Washington Wizards session scheduled for Thursday and a likely meeting with the Memphis Grizzlies upcoming.</p>
<p>He described leadership and winning culture as his primary contributions at the next level, beyond his scoring and rebounding ability.</p>
<p>"Being able to kind of impact winning and impact the culture of the team is going to be something I really bring from day one," Wilson said. "I would be happy to go to any team that believes in me."</p></description>

      <description><p>Duke big man Cameron Boozer arrived at the NBA Draft Combine Wednesday making his case for the top pick, pointing to his feel for the game and competitive instincts as the skills most likely to translate immediately to the professional level.</p>
<p>"I think my mind, for sure," Boozer told reporters when asked what he brings to an NBA team. "My feel for the game is elite. My competitiveness, my will to win, I think those are the biggest things that are going to translate."</p>
<p>Boozer is among four prospects competing for the top selection at next month's draft. The Washington Wizards hold the No. 1 pick, followed by the Utah Jazz at second, the Memphis Grizzlies at third and the Chicago Bulls at fourth.</p>
<p>The first four picks is expected to be some combination of Boozer, AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson and Caleb Wilson.    <item>
      <title>Sam Presti Uses 'Method Acting'-Like Process To Evaluate How Players Fit Thunder Culture</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285617/Sam-Presti-Uses-Method-Acting-Like-Process-To-Evaluate-How-Players-Fit-Thunder-Culture</link>
      <description><p>The Oklahoma City Thunder have swept the Phoenix Suns and Los Angeles Lakers in the first two rounds of the playoffs and enter the Western Conference finals as <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/news/nba-championship-betting-odds/">the clear favorite to repeat as NBA champions</a>.</p>
<p>General manager Sam Presti, who dismantled the roster after the Russell Westbrook and Paul George era and rebuilt it from scratch in a way that has become the model for many other franchises across the NBA. The trade that sent George to the Los Angeles Clippers in 2019 yielded Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and five first-round draft picks.</p>
<p>Presti's evaluation process extends beyond basketball. When Chet Holmgren was finishing his only season at Gonzaga, Presti traveled to Spokane not to assess his game but to observe how he carried himself around teammates and coaches. Holmgren had no idea at the time.</p>
<p>"I found out he wasn't paying any attention to anything having to do with basketball," Holmgren said. "I didn't realize it had that level of impact. Now I know he was doing his Mr. Miyagi mind-reading routine on me. And apparently, I passed the test, so I'll just keep being myself."</p>
<p>Mark Daigneault describes Presti's process as something close to method acting.</p>
<p>"As he's evaluating the player, Sam's really imagining that he's looking at them on the team," Daigneault says. "It's like method acting. He really goes deep. He's really trying to evaluate what the guy would be like walking around our building. One time we were discussing a player, and he said to me, 'All the guys are standing there watching film before practice, and I'm just imagining him standing there with them, and I just don't see him fitting in.' And that was it."</p>
<p>Gilgeous-Alexander's summary of Presti's track record is brief: "He's not wrong very many times."</p>
<p>The culture Presti has assembled reflects that standard at every level. Inside the Thunder's practice facility, basketballs are perfectly aligned on their racks, water bottles face label-out in the refrigerators and folded towels are stacked with their stripes in identical formation. The precision is not incidental. It reflects a broader organizational philosophy built around removing variables and engineering consistency.</p>
<p>"There are all kinds of constraints," Daigneault said. "There's a constraint on minutes, there's a constraint on roster spots, there's a constraint with the salary cap. They know I only have so many minutes. They know I can only start five guys. They're smart; they get it. But there's no constraint on the investment you can make when they come in the building every day and making sure you deliver a first-class experience to every single player every single day."</p>
<p>According to Holmgren, the standard does not require players to change who they are.</p>
<p>"There's a standard everybody here conforms to," Holmgren said, "but I don't think anybody who is brought in here has to make changes to themselves or how they go about things. Everyone has innate principles to their lives that we all share."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 13 May 2026 17:10:57 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285617/Sam-Presti-Uses-Method-Acting-Like-Process-To-Evaluate-How-Players-Fit-Thunder-Culture</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Presti_Sam_okc_230613.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>Dylan Harper Emerging As Playoff Star After Buying Into Role During Rookie Season</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285614/Dylan-Harper-Emerging-As-Playoff-Star-After-Buying-Into-Role-During-Rookie-Season</link>
      <description><p>San Antonio Spurs rookie Dylan Harper is delivering one of the most composed postseason performances by a first-year player in recent memory. The 20-year-old guard has outscored every bench player in this year's playoffs while helping the Spurs take a 3-2 series lead over the Minnesota Timberwolves, and the basketball world is starting to take notice.</p>
<p>Harper leads all reserves in postseason scoring with 136 points on 54.9% shooting, ahead of even former Sixth Man of the Year winner Naz Reid. San Antonio has outscored opponents by 73 points with Harper on the floor across 10 playoff games.</p>
<p>"If he played for any other team in the league," fellow Spurs rookie Carter Bryant said, "he'd be starting and probably be winning the Rookie of the Year right now. And to see how he's sacrificed and bought into his role, it's amazing."</p>
<p>The No. 2 overall pick out of Rutgers in 2025 spent his rookie season coming off the bench, starting just four of 69 regular season games. With De'Aaron Fox running point and Victor Wembanyama commanding touches, Harper learned to be productive without the ball. The adjustment has made him more dangerous, not less.</p>
<p>His versatility gives head coach Mitch Johnson genuine lineup flexibility. Harper can defend and contribute at any perimeter position and even some power forward situations. Six of the ten Spurs lineups with the best plus-minus numbers this postseason include him.</p>
<p>"The most impressive for me ... the way he controls his body," Wembanyama said of Harper. "His body awareness. Whether it's on drives or on jumps or relocation in the air. And you can see that in a variety of actions. Offensively, but also on rebounds and stuff, and on steals. It's quite impressive."</p>
<p>Harper's calm under pressure draws comparisons to Kawhi Leonard's early playoff appearances with San Antonio in 2012. Leonard arrived as an unassuming rookie who kept making winning plays against seasoned veterans. The similarities with Harper are less about style and more about maturity and the way high-stakes moments reveal capability rather than inexperience.</p>
<p>The Spurs are one win from a conference finals matchup with the defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder. If Harper's first ten playoff games are any indication of what lies ahead, San Antonio's future may be arriving ahead of schedule.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 13 May 2026 12:44:05 EDT</pubDate>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Harper_Dylan_sas_260502.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Terry Rozier, NBA Face Arbitrator Again Over $26.6M Salary Dispute</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285613/Terry-Rozier-NBA-Face-Arbitrator-Again-Over-$266M-Salary-Dispute</link>
      <description><p>The NBA and former Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier appeared before an arbitrator last month for a second time to dispute whether he is entitled to his $26.6 million salary while facing federal charges tied to an alleged sports gambling scheme, three sources briefed on the hearing told The Athletic.</p>
<p>The NBA placed Rozier on administrative leave in October, days after federal prosecutors in the Eastern District of New York charged him with two felony counts of conspiracy to commit wire fraud and conspiracy to commit money laundering. The league withheld his salary and placed the funds in escrow.</p>
<p>An arbitrator ruled this past winter, following a grievance filed by the NBPA, that Rozier was entitled to payment despite the charges. The league challenged that outcome this spring, arguing that Rozier's bond conditions prevented him from playing and that his salary should be reduced accordingly.</p>
<p>Rozier has pleaded not guilty. Prosecutors allege he informed a friend in advance that he would exit a March 2023 game early, with that friend then selling the information to gamblers who wagered on Rozier's player prop bets. A superseding indictment is expected this month.</p>
<p>"We have developed evidence that Mr. Rozier solicited and accepted a bribe," Eastern District prosecutor Kaitlin Farrell said during an April hearing.</p>
<p>Rozier did not appear in a single game this season after his October 23 arrest. Miami waived him last month. Former NBA player Damon Jones, one of six co-defendants in the case, has already pleaded guilty.</p>
<p>The case is part of a broader reckoning for the NBA over gambling-related misconduct. The league has relaunched its own internal investigation, requesting documents from implicated teams. Major League Baseball faces a parallel situation, with pitchers Emmanuel Clase and Luis Ortiz under indictment for allegedly manipulating pitch outcomes to benefit sports bettors. MLB and its players union agreed in March that both pitchers would remain on unpaid, non-disciplinary leave ahead of an expected November trial.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 13 May 2026 12:19:05 EDT</pubDate>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Rozier_Terry_mia_240226.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>LeBron James Will Evaluate How Lakers Show Interest In Retaining Him</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285612/LeBron-James-Will-Evaluate-How-Lakers-Show-Interest-In-Retaining-Him</link>
      <description><p>LeBron James is weighing his future with the Los Angeles Lakers this offseason, and people familiar with his thinking say the organization's approach in demonstrating genuine interest will carry as much weight as the contract figure itself. How the Lakers frame any offer, not just the dollar amount, could determine whether James returns for another season.</p>
<p>"The Lakers can't just offer James a number. They have to offer a why behind that number," an agent with prior clients on L.A.'s roster told ESPN. "Their plan for any discount would have to be coherent."</p>
<p>The stakes of that dynamic are sharpened by what sources describe as a pattern of perceived slights. One source close to James told ESPN the organization tried to "push him out the door" following the trade for Luka Doncic. James left Crypto.com Arena after at least one game this season without changing clothes, still simmering over what he viewed as the franchise repeatedly taking him for granted.</p>
<p>The Lakers' internal salary discussions have added another layer of tension. During games this season, team officials ran an informal exercise in which an observer was asked to watch James play and assign a market value to that anonymous player, setting aside his name and legacy entirely. The full midlevel exception sits at $14.1 million, but figures of $20 million, $25 million and $30 million were also floated.</p>
<p>For James, as one of the best players in NBA history continuing to play at an All-Star level being evaluated as a nameless 6-foot-9 skills player could reasonably be read as a dismissal of everything he has meant to the franchise.</p>
<p>Yet those close to him say the door remains open.</p>
<p>"I think it's up to the Lakers," a source who attended Game 4 against Oklahoma City told ESPN. "He loves it in L.A."</p>
<p>James echoed that tone directly. When asked after the Thunder series whether any friction remained between the two sides, he was brief and direct.</p>
<p>"There's no static from me," James told ESPN.</p></description>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/James_LeBron_lal_251210.jpg" />

   <item>
      <title>Paul George Has Rebuilt Trade Value, Making Him Easier For 76ers To Move Than Joel Embiid</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285607/Paul-George-Has-Rebuilt-Trade-Value-Making-Him-Easier-For-76ers-To-Move-Than-Joel-Embiid</link>
      <description><p>While Joel Embiid's injury history and contract structure make him among the hardest stars to trade in the league, Paul George has steadily recovered his market value and is now viewed by rival executives as the more moveable piece if the Philadelphia 76ers decide to rebuild around Tyrese Maxey and VJ Edgecombe.</p>
<p>Multiple executives indicated that Embiid's persistent availability issues make any serious trade effort difficult to structure. No definitive push to move the center is expected this offseason, regardless of who leads the front office after they parted ways with Daryl Morey.</p>
<p>George presents a fundamentally different profile. At 36, he carries one guaranteed season at $54.1 million for 2026-27, followed by a $56.6 million player option in 2027-28. That structure creates room for a potential suitor to offer a multi-year extension at a lower annual figure if George were willing to bypass the option year.</p>
<p>After serving a 25-game suspension for a banned substance, George shot 49.2% from three-point range during the postseason. The shooting, playmaking, and wing defense he demonstrated suggest meaningful value remains.</p>
<p>The Trae Young trade offers a pricing benchmark. The Atlanta Hawks received only two team-friendly contracts, CJ McCollum and Corey Kispert, and no draft compensation in that deal. If George's cost lands in a similar range, the number of interested teams grows considerably.</p>
<p>Several executives have noted that Embiid's three-year, $188 million extension, which begins next season, was viewed internally as the more consequential front-office error committed by Morey. The extension was structured in part to secure George's free-agent commitment in 2024, a sequence that made sense at the time but now leaves Philadelphia carrying two complicated contracts with limited postseason results to show for either.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 13 May 2026 07:41:48 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285607/Paul-George-Has-Rebuilt-Trade-Value-Making-Him-Easier-For-76ers-To-Move-Than-Joel-Embiid</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/George_Paul_phi_250226.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA's New Lottery Reform Expected To Make Teams More Reluctant To Trade Future Firsts</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285606/NBAs-New-Lottery-Reform-Expected-To-Make-Teams-More-Reluctant-To-Trade-Future-Firsts</link>
      <description><p>The volume of <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/detailed">future first round picks</a> exchanged for star players is expected to decline in the near term as a result of the NBA's new lottery reform. Teams are choosing to wait and understand the full implications of the new system before committing draft capital.</p>
<p>The NBA's proposed "3-2-1" lottery format is headed for a Board of Governors vote on May 28 and is widely expected to pass. Beginning next season, teams finishing fourth through tenth would each hold an equal 8.1% shot at the top overall pick. The expanded lottery would include 16 teams, drawing in more than half the league.</p>
<p>That wider participation is the core issue as when more teams carry a realistic chance at a franchise-altering selection, the incentive to hold picks rather than trade them grows considerably. Front offices that once moved future assets freely are now treating them with greater care.</p>
<p>The looming trade market for Milwaukee Bucks forward Giannis Antetokounmpo will illustrate the state of the market. As his future in Milwaukee moves toward resolution, teams assembling offers must weigh precisely how much draft capital makes sense to surrender under a lottery system that rewards depth of misfortune less than it once did.</p>
<p>The experience of the Indiana Pacers from Sunday's lottery serves as a cautionary example leaguewide. The Pacers lost their top-four protected first-round pick to the Los Angeles Clippers, a condition attached to February's Ivica Zubac trade, after drawing the fifth slot. The outcome has reinforced the stakes of attaching picks to trades.</p>
<p>Franchises that may never feel compelled to truly tank and bottom out could still hold valuable picks after just missing out on the playoffs.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/tradeable">Teams are currently limited in the number of picks they can trade due to the Stepien rule</a>, but they may now put additional guardrails on themselves out of fear of trading away a future top pick.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Wed, 13 May 2026 07:29:29 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285606/NBAs-New-Lottery-Reform-Expected-To-Make-Teams-More-Reluctant-To-Trade-Future-Firsts</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Antetokounmpo_Giannis_mil_251210.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Bob Myers To Return To Advisory Role For Sixers After Naming Daryl Morey Successor</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285605/Bob-Myers-To-Return-To-Advisory-Role-For-Sixers-After-Naming-Daryl-Morey-Successor</link>
      <description><p>Bob Myers will return to his role as the president of Harris Blitzer Sports & Entertainment after he names a new president of basketball operations for the Philadelphia 76ers, according to Jake Fischer of The People's Insider. </p>
<p>Myers is temporarily in charge of the team's basketball operations as he <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285599/76ers-Part-Ways-With-Daryl-Morey-Retain-Nick-Nurse-As-Head-Coach">searches for Daryl Morey's successor</a>. </p>
<p>Myers will interview both internal and external candidates for the position. </p>
<p>It is too early in the process to name a favorite for the role. </p>
<p>Fischer adds that current executive director of the NBA Players Association Andre Iguodala will not be a candidate for the Sixers' job. </p></description>

      <title>Sixers Ownership Approved Of Jared McCain Trade</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285604/Sixers-Ownership-Approved-Of-Jared-McCain-Trade</link>
      <description><p>Philadelphia Sixers ownership approved of <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/283740/Thunder-Acquire-Jared-McCain-From-Sixers">Daryl Morey trading Jared McCain</a> to the Oklahoma City Thunder for a 2026 first-round pick and three second-round picks, according to Jake Fischer of The People's Insider.</p>
<p>Morey received criticism following the trade with McCain averaging 10.4 points per game with the Thunder.</p>
<p>Morey said at the time that the Sixers "<a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285499/Jared-McCain-Definitely-Hurt-By-Sold-High-Comment-After-Trade-From-Sixers">sold high</a>" on McCain. </p>
<p>With Sixers ownership <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285599/76ers-Part-Ways-With-Daryl-Morey-Retain-Nick-Nurse-As-Head-Coach">parting ways with Morey</a> on Tuesday, team execs feel confident in their ability to select a quality player with the No. 22 pick in the upcoming draft. </p></description>

      <title>Spurs-Timberwolves Game 4 Averages 7.9M Viewers</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285603/Spurs-Timberwolves-Game-4-Averages-79M-Viewers</link>
      <description><p>Sunday's Game 4 between the San Antonio Spurs and Minnesota Timberwolves averaged 7.9 million viewers across Nielsen and Adobe Analytics.</p>
<p>It is the second most-watched game in the playoffs, only trailing Game 7 between the Sixers and Celtics.</p>
<p>Friday's Game 3 averaged 5.25 million viewers on Prime Video, making it the largest audience for the streamer this season.</p>
<p>Game 2 of the series averaged 5.2 million viewers on ESPN, the most-watched game of the playoffs on the cable network.</p>
<p>Saturday's matchup between the Pistons and Cavs averaged 4.9 million viewers on NBC, down 8% from last year. </p></description>
